SandboxHome.heading: A mobile design sandbox for Stashy.
SandboxHome.description: Build app screens here with the same tokens, rhythm, and bilingual behavior used by the native product.

intro.title: Know what you can safely spend today.
intro.description: Stashy is built around one number: today’s safe-to-spend amount, plus what tomorrow looks like if you drift off pace.

daily.title: Daily Rate
daily.explanationTrack: You still have room inside today’s safe spending limit.
daily.explanationOverspent: You’ve spent past today’s safe limit, so tomorrow’s rate is now under pressure.
daily.overBySubtext: Variable budget exhausted for this month

major.title: Major Expenses
major.warning: Major expenses are high this month

navPlaceholders.tracker: Tracker flows will open here without leaving the mobile sandbox.
navPlaceholders.history: History now lives on its own screen with filters and totals.
navPlaceholders.analytics: Analytics will reuse the same budget rhythm with richer charts.
navPlaceholders.settings: Settings stay close so locale and theme can be tested quickly.

drawer.preview: This interaction is ready for the next implementation pass.

add.title: Add transaction
add.description: Choose the kind of money movement you want to record.

spend.label: Log spending
spend.description: Record a normal day-to-day expense that should count against today’s allowance.
spend.amountHint: Enter the amount that should count against today’s spending room.
spend.impact: This lowers what remains safe to spend today and can trigger tomorrow-impact messaging if you go over.
spend.next: The form should ask for amount, payment method, and whether this is a normal variable expense.
spend.formIntro: Capture a normal variable expense and immediately preview whether it keeps you on track or pushes tomorrow down.
spend.notePlaceholder: What did you spend this on?
spend.previewTrack: This keeps you within today’s safe spending limit.
spend.previewOverspent: This pushes you past today’s safe limit and lowers tomorrow’s room.
spend.responseTrack: {amount} was added as everyday spending, and Home stays on track.
spend.responseOverspent: {amount} was added as spending, and Home switches to overspent.

receive.label: Add received income
receive.description: Record money that increases this month’s variable budget for the days left.
receive.amountHint: Enter the amount that should increase this month’s available room.
receive.impact: Received income raises the month’s effective variable budget and can lift the daily rate for the days that remain.
receive.next: The form should ask for amount, payment method, and classify it as received money rather than a transfer or injection.
receive.formIntro: Capture money that genuinely increases this month’s variable budget rather than only offsetting a single day.
receive.notePlaceholder: Where did this money come from?
receive.preview: This lifts the month’s variable room and supports a stronger daily rate.
receive.response: {amount} was added as received income, and Home moves back to the safer on-track state.

injection.label: Add budget injection
injection.description: Top up the month on purpose when you need breathing room after going over budget.
injection.amountHint: Enter the top-up amount you want to add back into the month.
injection.impact: A budget injection deliberately gives the month more room, which helps recover the daily rate after pressure or emergency states.
injection.next: The form should make the recovery intent explicit so this action feels different from ordinary received income.
injection.formIntro: Capture a deliberate rescue or top-up so the interaction feels distinct from ordinary incoming money.
injection.notePlaceholder: Why are you topping up this month?
injection.preview: This restores breathing room and supports the daily rate for the remaining days.
injection.response: {amount} was added as a budget injection, and Home returns to a more stable state.

major.label: Add major purchase
major.description: Record a large one-time expense that should lower the rate for the rest of the month.
major.amountHint: Enter the purchase amount that should pressure the rest of the month.
major.impact: Major purchases do not count as normal daily spending. They reduce the month’s effective budget and push the remaining daily rate down.
major.next: The confirmation should preview the new daily rate before saving so the consequence feels immediate and clear.
major.formIntro: Capture a one-time major purchase separately so it changes the month without pretending it was ordinary daily spend.
major.preview: This keeps the purchase separate from daily spending while pushing the rest of the month harder.
major.response: {amount} was added as a major purchase, and the major-expense state stays visible on Home.

filter.title: Filter history
filter.description: Narrow the transaction list by movement type.

help.title: Daily Rate help
help.description: Understand the core ideas behind today’s spending limit.

todayRate.title: Today’s Rate
todayRate.description: This is your reality-based spending target for today, not just a monthly average.

remaining.title: Remaining Today
remaining.description: This tells you how much room you still have before today starts hurting tomorrow.

major.title: Major Purchases
major.description: Major expenses don’t count as normal daily spending. They lower the month’s effective budget instead.

tomorrow.title: Tomorrow impact
tomorrow.onTrack: When you stay on track, tomorrow doesn’t need a warning card.
tomorrow.overspent: When you overspend today, Stashy shows how much tomorrow’s safe amount drops.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Switch theme or language while keeping the mobile preview in place.
settings.introHint: Keep dismissable onboarding surfaces re-toggleable here so newcomer and returning states can both be reviewed.

fixed.title: Fixed payments
fixed.description: Review every fixed commitment due this month.

history.title: History
history.description: Review recent spending and incoming money.

History.title: History
History.emptyDescription: Change the filters or widen the date range to bring history back in view.

filter.title: Filter history
filter.description: Refine one history view by type, direction, payment method, and time range.

overBudgetCallout.title: {count, plural, one {1 budget envelope is over} other {# budget envelopes are over}}
overBudgetCallout.body: {name} exceeded its envelope by {amount}. The overage settles from your variable budget — your total fixed commitment is still within budget.
overBudgetCallout.bodyMulti: These envelopes exceeded their limits this month. The overage settles from your variable budget — your total fixed commitment is still within budget.

budgets.transactionPreview: Transactions stay collapsed by default for this preview.

major.warning: Major expenses are high this month
major.summaryTitle: Major purchases are reshaping this month.
major.summaryDescription: {count} major purchases have already pulled {amount} out of the month’s flexible budget.

history.emptyDescription: Change the filters or widen the date range to bring history back in view.

empty.monthlyDescription: Add things like rent or subscriptions to track them automatically.
empty.budgetDescription: Create a spending bucket to watch category limits during the month.
empty.majorDescription: Record large one-time purchases like appliances or medical bills here.

fixed.title: Add to Fixed
fixed.description: Choose whether this is a recurring payment or a budget transaction.
fixed.monthlyPaymentDescription: Add a recurring bill so it appears with upcoming fixed obligations.
fixed.monthlyPaymentNameHint: Name the recurring bill you want to track each month.
fixed.monthlyPaymentAmountHint: Enter the amount that should appear in Monthly.
fixed.monthlyPaymentDueHint: Choose the day this bill should appear as due.
fixed.budgetTransactionDescription: Log a bucketed fixed transaction so the category progress updates immediately.
fixed.budgetTransactionAmountHint: Enter the amount that should update this fixed bucket now.
fixed.budgetTransactionNoteHint: Add a short note if the transaction name matters in History.
fixed.monthlyPaymentEffect: {amount} will appear in Monthly as a pending payment due on day {dueDay}.
fixed.budgetTransactionEffect: {amount} will increase the {category} bucket and show up in History right away.
fixed.budgetTransactionPlaceholder: What was this fixed budget spend for?

major.title: Add Major Expense
major.formDescription: Keep this expense separate from everyday spending so the month reflects its true pressure.
major.nameHint: Name the purchase the way you want it to appear in Major and History.
major.amountHint: Enter the amount that should reshape the rest of this month.
major.effectRegular: {amount} will appear in Major and History as a one-time month-shaping expense.
major.effectLarge: {amount} will appear in Major as a large purchase and reinforce the warning state.

history.title: History is read-only
history.description: Use filters to inspect past movement.

filter.title: Filter history
filter.description: Refine one history view by type, direction, payment method, and time range.

month.description: Jump to another saved month from here.

pacing.narrative: You've used {budgetUsed}% of your budget with {monthProgress}% of the month gone - {descriptor}.
pacing.barLabel: Budget pace compared with month progress

projection.title: Projection
projection.subtitle: Where this month is likely heading if nothing changes.
projection.positiveNarrative: If you keep this pace, you'll finish {month} {amount} under budget.
projection.negativeNarrative: At this rate, you'll overspend by {amount} by end of month. Consider pulling back on variable spending.

shaping.title: What’s Shaping This Month
shaping.subtitle: A quick read on the few forces that matter most right now.
shaping.fixedOverBudget: Fixed spending is over budget by {amount}, with {manualCount} manual bucket over limit and {paidCount}/{totalCount} auto payments already cleared.
shaping.fixedWithinBudget: Fixed spending is within the {budget} plan, and {paidCount}/{totalCount} auto payments are already cleared.
shaping.majorDragging: {count} major purchases are pulling {amount} out of the month.
shaping.majorNone: No major purchases are dragging this month.
shaping.leftToWorkWith: After income, fixed pressure, and major purchases, you still have {amount} left to work with.
shaping.missingFromPlan: After income, fixed pressure, and major purchases, you are missing {amount} from plan.

comparison.title: vs Last Month
comparison.largeLess: Fewer large purchases than last month
comparison.verdictBetter: You're trending better than March across the metrics that moved.
comparison.verdictMostlyBetter: April looks better than March overall, with stronger savings and lighter large purchases.
comparison.verdictMostlyWorse: April is slipping behind March on the metrics that moved.
comparison.verdictWorse: April is tracking below March on both key comparison metrics.
comparison.verdictMixed: Mixed month - one metric improved while another moved the wrong way.
comparison.baseRateReason: Base rate unchanged - fixed expenses stayed the same
comparison.largePurchasesMessage: Fewer large purchases than last month
comparison.emptyDescription: No previous month data yet. Close {month} to unlock comparison.

upgrade.title: Unlock Analytics
upgrade.description: See your spending pace, monthly trends, and savings projections all in one place.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.subtitle: Manage your profile, security, and budgeting defaults.

budget.title: Monthly Budget
budget.subtitle: Set your target budget for the month

boosts.title: Budget Management
boosts.subtitle: Temporarily increase your monthly budget
boosts.empty: No active boosts. Tap Add to create one.

guide.title: How to Use Stashy

about.description: Track spending, fixed costs, and goals across devices.

drawer.editProfileDescription: Update the profile details that show across your account.
drawer.changeBudgetDescription: Adjust the monthly budget target used across the app.
drawer.addBoostDescription: Create a temporary increase with a clear expiry date.
drawer.methodDescription: Choose the label and icon that best match this payment method.

Fallback.notFoundDescription: This route does not exist yet.
Fallback.errorDescription: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
